results.name: rat king
results.description: A group of rats with their tails entangled. Don't google it.

results.name: ratonhnhaké꞉ton
results.description: Main character of Assassin's Creed 3, also known as Connor

results.name: raugrin
results.description:
The blue-red-white-aligned triome of Ikoria.
Volcanic mountains surrounding white-sand beaches along the coastline.

results.name: ravnica faction symbol
results.description: Faction symbols found on Ravnica.

results.name: razor fields
results.description: A landmark on Mirrodin consisting of fields of razor-sharp grass, near and overlapping the Glimmervoid.

results.name: razorkin
results.description: Razorkin are former survivors on the plane Duskmourn, who now hunt the remaining survivors for their amusement.

results.name: real life animal name
results.description: Card names that match real world english animal names. "Manta ray" is accepted, while "Hurr jackal" is not. Adjectives can disqualify a card unless it is a part of their name. "Giant ladybug" is a species name and therefore accepted, while "Venomous dragonfly" is not.

results.name: real life historical figure
results.description: Depictions of Historical figures from earth

results.name: real life location
results.description: Art depicting actual real life sights and lardmarks, such as the international lands.

results.name: real life reference
results.description: For art depicting or referencing real life people, locations, landmarks, and events. This is not for references to stories, fables, legends, and mythology. See also [fiction reference](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/artwork/fiction-reference).

results.name: reanimate
results.description: Return permanent cards from the graveyard to the battlefield.

results.name: reanimate-cast
results.description: Cast a permanent spell from your graveyard.

results.name: reanimate copy
results.description: "Brings back" a copy of a permanent card in a graveyard instead of the original. Could be a token copy, could be turning another card into a copy of it.

results.name: reanimate-creature
results.description: Return creature cards currently in the graveyard to the battlefield. Not to be confused with [cheat death](/tags/card/cheat-death), which interacts right as the creature dies.

results.name: reanimate-from-opponent
results.description: Cards that specifically have the ability to reanimate cards opponents have, if not any card

results.name: reanimate-self
results.description: These cards can return themselves to the battlefield if they're sitting in the graveyard. Not to be confused with [cheat death-self](cheat-death-self), where the card returns immediately after dying.

results.name: rearing
results.description: A normally quadrupedal animal raised up to stand on its hind legs temporarily.

results.name: rebecca guay
results.description: Rebecca Guay often uses herself as a model in her illustrations, which means a lot of card art contains self-portraits of her.

results.name: recasting commander matters
results.description: These cards care about you recasting your commander, or the number of times it's been cast.

results.name: reclined
results.description: Laying back with the back supported.

results.name: recurring suspend
results.description: Cards that have suspend and, upon resolving, suspend themselves once more.

results.name: recursion
results.description: Recursion, fractals, and art depicting itself. For the fractal creature type, see [quandrix fractal](/tags/artwork/quandrix-fractal).

results.name: recursion
results.description: Get stuff back from your graveyard to use it again. See: reanimate (to battlefield), regrowth (to hand), and restock (to library).

results.name: recursion from exile
results.description: Brings itself or other cards you own back from exile, regardless of what effect might have put them there. For effects that do this to your opponent's cards, see [processing](https://tagger.scryfall.com/tags/card/processing).

results.name: recycle
results.description: Sacrifice a thing to get another thing back from the graveyard.
